children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of activities conducted under this chapter, the Secretary shall identify and have in effect a contract with a consensus-based entity, such as the National Quality Forum, that meets the requirements described in subsection (c). Such contract shall provide that the entity will perform the duties described in subsection (b).

children.heading: Timing for first contract
children.label: 2 Timing for first contract
children.text: As soon as practicable after , the Secretary shall enter into the first contract under paragraph (1).July 15, 2008

children.heading: Period of contract
children.label: 3 Period of contract
children.text: A contract under paragraph (1) shall be for a period of 4 years (except as may be renewed after a subsequent bidding process).

children.heading: Competitive procedures
children.label: 4 Competitive procedures
children.text: Competitive procedures (as defined in ) shall be used to enter into a contract under paragraph (1).section 132 of title 41

children.label: i
children.text: that address the health care provided to patients with prevalent, high-cost chronic diseases;

children.label: ii
children.text: with the greatest potential for improving the quality, efficiency, and patient-centeredness of health care; and

children.label: iii
children.text: that may be implemented rapidly due to existing evidence, standards of care, or other reasons; and

children.label: i
children.text: may assist consumers and patients in making informed health care decisions;

children.label: ii
children.text: address health disparities across groups and areas; and

children.label: iii
children.text: address the continuum of care a patient receives, including services furnished by multiple health care providers or practitioners and across multiple settings.

children.label: A
children.text: is evidence-based, reliable, valid, verifiable, relevant to enhanced health outcomes, actionable at the caregiver level, feasible to collect and report, and responsive to variations in patient characteristics, such as health status, language capabilities, race or ethnicity, and income level; and

children.label: B
children.text: is consistent across types of health care providers, including hospitals and physicians.

children.heading: Maintenance of measures
children.label: 3 Maintenance of measures
children.text: The entity shall establish and implement a process to ensure that measures endorsed under paragraph (2) are updated (or retired if obsolete) as new evidence is developed.

children.heading: Removal of measures
children.label: 4 Removal of measures
children.text: The entity may provide input to the Secretary on quality and efficiency measures described in paragraph (7)(B) that could be considered for removal.

children.heading: Annual report to Congress and the Secretary; secretarial publication and comment
children.label: 5 Annual report to Congress and the Secretary; secretarial publication and comment
I
children.text: the implementation of quality measurement initiatives under this chapter and the coordination of such initiatives with quality initiatives implemented by other payers;

children.label: II
children.text: the recommendations made under paragraph (1);

children.label: III
children.text: the performance by the entity of the duties required under the contract entered into with the Secretary under subsection (a);

children.label: IV
children.text: gaps in endorsed quality measures, which shall include measures that are within priority areas identified by the Secretary under the national strategy established under , and where quality measures are unavailable or inadequate to identify or address such gaps;section 280j of this title

children.label: V
children.text: areas in which evidence is insufficient to support endorsement of quality measures in priority areas identified by the Secretary under the national strategy established under and where targeted research may address such gaps; andsection 280j of this title

children.label: VI
children.text: the matters described in clauses (i) and (ii) of paragraph (7)(A).

children.label: I
children.text: annual revenues of the entity (including any government funding, private sector contributions, grants, membership revenues, and investment revenue);

children.label: II
children.text: annual expenses of the entity (including grants paid, benefits paid, salaries or other compensation, fundraising expenses, and overhead costs); and

children.label: III
children.text: a breakdown of the amount awarded per contracted task order and the specific projects funded in each task order assigned to the entity.

children.label: I
children.text: specifically identifying any modifications to the disclosure of interests and conflicts of interests for committees, work groups, task forces, and advisory panels of the entity; and

children.label: II
children.text: information on external stakeholder participation in the duties of the entity under this section (including complete rosters for all committees, work groups, task forces, and advisory panels funded through government contracts, descriptions of relevant interests and any conflicts of interest for members of all committees, work groups, task forces, and advisory panels, and the total percentage by health care sector of all convened committees, work groups, task forces, and advisory panels.2So in original. Probably should be “panels).”2

children.heading: Secretarial review and publication of annual report
children.label: B Secretarial review and publication of annual report
i
children.text: review such report; and

children.label: ii
children.text: publish such report in the Federal Register, together with any comments of the Secretary on such report.

children.heading: Review and endorsement of episode grouper under the physician feedback program
children.label: 6 Review and endorsement of episode grouper under the physician feedback program
children.text: The entity shall provide for the review and, as appropriate, the endorsement of the episode grouper developed by the Secretary under . Such review shall be conducted on an expedited basis.section 1395w–4(n)(9)(A) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: such measures that have been endorsed by the entity; and

children.label: II
children.text: such measures that have not been considered for endorsement by such entity but are used or proposed to be used by the Secretary for the collection or reporting of quality and efficiency measures; and

children.label: ii
children.text: national priorities (as identified under ) for improvement in population health and in the delivery of health care services for consideration under the national strategy established under .section 280j of this titlesection 280j of this title

children.label: I
children.text: for use pursuant to sections 1395f(i)(5)(D), 1395(i)(7), 1395(t)(17), 1395w–4(k)(2)(C), 1395cc(k)(3), 1395rr(h)(2)(A)(iii), 1395ww(b)(3)(B)(viii), 1395ww(j)(7)(D), 1395ww(m)(5)(D), 1395ww()(2), 1395ww(s)(4)(D), and 1395fff(b)(3)(B)(v) of this title;llo3See References in Text note below.3

children.label: II
children.text: for use in reporting performance information to the public; and

children.label: III
children.text: for use in health care programs other than for use under this chapter.

children.heading: Exclusion
children.label: ii Exclusion
children.text: Data sets (such as the outcome and assessment information set for home health services and the minimum data set for skilled nursing facility services) that are used for purposes of classification systems used in establishing payment rates under this subchapter shall not be quality and efficiency measures described in this subparagraph.

children.heading: Requirement for transparency in process
children.label: C Requirement for transparency in process

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In convening multi-stakeholder groups under subparagraph (A) with respect to the selection of quality and efficiency measures, the entity shall provide for an open and transparent process for the activities conducted pursuant to such convening.

children.heading: Selection of organizations participating in multi-stakeholder groups
children.label: ii Selection of organizations participating in multi-stakeholder groups
children.text: The process described in clause (i) shall ensure that the selection of representatives comprising such groups provides for public nominations for, and the opportunity for public comment on, such selection.

children.heading: Multi-stakeholder group defined
children.label: D Multi-stakeholder group defined
children.text: In this paragraph, the term “multi-stakeholder group” means, with respect to a quality and efficiency measure, a voluntary collaborative of organizations representing a broad group of stakeholders interested in or affected by the use of such quality and efficiency measure.

children.heading: Transmission of multi-stakeholder input
children.label: 8 Transmission of multi-stakeholder input
children.text: Not later than February 1 of each year (beginning with 2012), the entity shall transmit to the Secretary the input of multi-stakeholder groups provided under paragraph (7).

children.label: A
children.text: during the period beginning on , and ending on , shall prioritize the endorsement of measures relating to maternal morbidity and mortality by the entity with a contract under subsection (a) in connection with endorsement of measures described in paragraph (2); andDecember 27, 2020December 31, 2023

children.label: B
children.text: on and after , may prioritize the endorsement of such measures by such entity.January 1, 2024

children.heading: Private nonprofit
children.label: 1 Private nonprofit
children.text: The entity is a private nonprofit entity governed by a board.

children.label: A
children.text: representatives of health plans and health care providers and practitioners or representatives of groups representing such health plans and health care providers and practitioners;

children.label: B
children.text: health care consumers or representatives of groups representing health care consumers; and

children.label: C
children.text: representatives of purchasers and employers or representatives of groups representing purchasers or employers.

children.label: A
children.text: urban health care issues;

children.label: B
children.text: safety net health care issues;

children.label: C
children.text: rural and frontier health care issues; and

children.label: D
children.text: health care quality and safety issues.

children.heading: Open and transparent
children.label: 4 Open and transparent
children.text: With respect to matters related to the contract with the Secretary under subsection (a), the entity conducts its business in an open and transparent manner and provides the opportunity for public comment on its activities.

children.heading: Voluntary consensus standards setting organization
children.label: 5 Voluntary consensus standards setting organization
children.text: The entity operates as a voluntary consensus standards setting organization as defined for purposes of section 12(d) of the National Technology Transfer and Advancement Act of 1995 () and Office of Management and Budget Revised Circular A–119 (published in the Federal Register on ).Public Law 104–113February 10, 1998

children.heading: Experience
children.label: 6 Experience
children.text: The entity has at least 4 years of experience in establishing national consensus standards.

children.heading: Membership fees
children.label: 7 Membership fees
children.text: If the entity requires a membership fee for participation in the functions of the entity, such fees shall be reasonable and adjusted based on the capacity of the potential member to pay the fee. In no case shall membership fees pose a barrier to the participation of individuals or groups with low or nominal resources to participate in the functions of the entity.

For purposes of carrying out this section, the Secretary shall provide for the transfer, from the Federal Hospital Insurance Trust Fund under and the Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Trust Fund under (in such proportion as the Secretary determines appropriate), of $10,000,000 to the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services Program Management Account for each of fiscal years 2009 through 2013. Amounts transferred under the preceding sentence shall remain available until expended.section 1395i of this titlesection 1395t of this title

For purposes of carrying out this section and (other than subsections (e) and (f)), the Secretary shall provide for the transfer, from the Federal Hospital Insurance Trust Fund under and the Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Trust Fund under , in such proportion as the Secretary determines appropriate, to the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services Program Management Account of $5,000,000 for fiscal year 2014, $30,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2015 through 2017, $7,500,000 for each of fiscal years 2018 and 2019, $20,000,000 for fiscal year 2020, $26,000,000 for fiscal year 2021, $20,000,000 for fiscal year 2022, $20,000,000 for fiscal year 2023, $14,030,000 for the period beginning on , and ending on , $13,300,000 for fiscal year 2026, and $15,100,000 for fiscal year 2027. Amounts transferred under the preceding sentence shall remain available until expended. Amounts transferred for each of fiscal years 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025, 2026, and 2027 shall be in addition to any unobligated funds transferred for a preceding fiscal year that are available under the preceding sentence.section 1395aaa–1 of this titlesection 1395i of this titlesection 1395t of this titleOctober 1, 2023September 30, 2025

children.heading: Annual report by Secretary to Congress
children.label: e Annual report by Secretary to Congress
A
children.text: A comprehensive plan that identifies the quality measurement needs of programs and initiatives of the Secretary and provides a strategy for using the entity with a contract under subsection (a) and any other entity the Secretary has contracted with or may contract with to perform work associated with to help meet those needs, specifically with respect to the programs under this subchapter and subchapter XIX.section 1395aaa–1 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: The amount of funding provided under subsection (d) for purposes of carrying out this section and that has been obligated by the Secretary, the amount of funding provided that has been expended, and the amount of funding provided that remains unobligated.section 1395aaa–1 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: With respect to the activities described under this section or , a description of how the funds described in paragraph (2) have been obligated or expended, including how much of that funding has been obligated or expended for work performed by the Secretary, the entity with a contract under subsection (a), and any other entity the Secretary has contracted with to perform work.section 1395aaa–1 of this title

children.label: D
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2)(B), a description of the activities for which the funds described in paragraph (2) were used, including task orders and activities assigned to the entity with a contract under subsection (a), activities performed by the Secretary, and task orders and activities assigned to any other entity the Secretary has contracted with to perform work related to carrying out .section 1395aaa–1 of this title

children.label: E
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2)(B), the amount of funding described in paragraph (2) that has been obligated or expended for each of the activities described in paragraph (4).

children.label: F
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2)(B), estimates for, and descriptions of, obligations and expenditures that the Secretary anticipates will be needed in the succeeding two year period to carry out each of the quality measurement activities required under this section and , including any obligations that will require funds to be expended in a future year.section 1395aaa–1 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: A comprehensive analysis detailing the ways in which the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services has addressed each of the recommendations set forth in the report by the Government Accountability Office (GAO–19–628) issued on , and titled “Health Care Quality: CMS Could More Effectively Ensure Its Quality Measurement Activities Promote Its Objectives”.September 19, 2019

children.label: I
children.text: any additional steps that the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services expects to take to address the findings and recommendations set forth in such report; and

children.label: II
children.text: the anticipated timing for such steps.

children.label: I
children.text: paragraph (1)(D) shall also include detailed information on each of the activities described in clause (ii);

children.label: II
children.text: paragraph (1)(E) shall also include detailed information on the specific amounts obligated or expended on each of the activities described in clause (ii); and

children.label: III
children.text: paragraph (1)(F) shall also include detailed information on the specific quality measurement activities required and future funding needed for each of the activities described in clause (ii).

children.label: I
children.text: Measure selection activities.

children.label: II
children.text: Measure development activities.

children.label: III
children.text: Public reporting activities.

children.label: IV
children.text: Education and outreach activities.

children.heading: Additional reporting by the Secretary to Congress
children.label: f Additional reporting by the Secretary to Congress

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: By not later than September 30 of each year (beginning with 2021), the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report on the amount of unobligated balances for appropriations relating to quality measurement. Such report shall include detailed plans on how the Secretary expects to expend such unobligated balances in the upcoming fiscal years.

children.heading: Separate report
children.label: 2 Separate report
children.text: The annual report required under paragraph (1) shall be separate from the annual report required under subsection (e).
